page: Microsoft Azure FX96-24ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family, virtualized via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 12 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The server is equipped with 1832.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 76.33 GB per physical core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, achieving a Geekbench single-core score of 2366.0, which places it in the top tier. Memory performance is also notable, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 187.06 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 118.92 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also register strong performance. Multi-core compute and database operations fall within the average range, reflecting the moderate core count relative to the massive memory footprint.

The hardware profile represents a specialized tradeoff, prioritizing extreme memory density over massive parallel core scaling. This resource distribution makes the instance highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not scale linearly with high core counts, avoiding the licensing or infrastructure costs of unnecessary vCPUs. The lack of local storage requires reliance on external premium storage. This configuration is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, heavy caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing.
description: Microsoft Azure FX96-24ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-intensive server from the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs across 12 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores clocked at 4.2 GHz, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache and AMX/AVX-512 instruction sets. The server is provisioned with 1832.0 GB of RAM, providing a high ratio of 76.33 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core performance with a Geekbench score of 2366.0, alongside strong memory bandwidth of 187.06 GB/sec for large-block reads. This configuration offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications by minimizing core-licensing overhead while maximizing RAM. It is suited for in-memory databases, large-scale enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX96-24ms_v2 is a memory-intensive server featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1832 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive x86 server featuring extreme RAM density and strong single-core performance for demanding in-memory enterprise workloads.

1832.0 GB of system memory providing 76.33 GB per physical core
Top-tier single-core performance with a Geekbench score of 2366.0

No local storage, requiring external premium storage solutions
